senses.definition: having or producing a loud, deep, resonant sound or voice
examples: The bisonant bell echoed across the valley.
Her bisonant laugh filled the room.

senses.definition: the plural form of bison, a large North American animal resembling a buffalo
examples: The bisons roamed the plains in herds.
Researchers studied the diet of bisons in the park.

senses.definition: of or relating to a bison; shaped like or resembling a bison
examples: A bisontine silhouette dominated the sculpture.
The bisontine landscape inspired artists.

senses.definition: a bone in the skull resembling a wedge-shaped bone; part of certain vertebrate skulls
examples: The bisphenoid contributes to the skull base.
Anatomy texts describe the bisphenoid's position.

senses.definition: a type of synthetic compound used in making plastics; often discussed as a potential hormone disruptor
examples: BPA is a common bisphenol used in polycarbonate plastics.
Researchers study the effects of bisphenols on health.

senses.definition: plural of bisphenol; multiple bisphenol compounds used in plastics manufacturing
examples: Manufacturers screen for various bisphenols in products.
Environmental tests detect several bisphenols.

senses.definition: a class of drugs that prevent bone breakdown; used to treat osteoporosis
examples: Doctors prescribe bisphosphonates to reduce fractures.
Common bisphosphonates include alendronate and risedronate.

senses.definition: the plural form of bisphosphonate; drugs that slow bone loss
examples: Bisphosphates are often prescribed for osteoporosis; however, side effects occur with long-term use.
Researchers compare different bisphosphonates for efficacy.

senses.definition: having two spinous processes or spines; in biology, referring to a two-pointed structure
examples: The bispinose plant features two spines along its stem.

senses.definition: having two spines or points; two-spined
examples: A bispinous plant has two sharp spines.
The bispinous feature helps deter herbivores.

senses.definition: a fungus producing two spores per fruiting body or a structure with two spores
examples: The mushroom exhibits a rare bispores arrangement.

senses.definition: producing two spores or having two spores; two-spored
examples: A bisporous mushroom forms two spores in each basidium.

senses.definition: a smooth, creamy soup made from seafood, usually lobster or crab
examples: She ordered lobster bisque as a starter.
The bisque was rich and flavorful.

senses.definition: plural of bisque; several bowls or varieties of creamy seafood soup
examples: The restaurant offers several bisques, including lobster and crab.
We shared two bisques as an appetizer.

senses.definition: a small biscuit or a thin, crisp wafer; also a small decorative badge in some contexts
examples: She wore a bisquette on her dress.
A bisquette snack accompanied the tea.

senses.definition: a genus name in biology; a plant species within the Bissabol group
examples: Bissabol species are studied for medicinal properties.

senses.definition: a genus of extinct or living aquatic plants; or a fossil genus in some classifications
examples: Fossils of Bissellia reveal ancient plant forms.

senses.definition: fourteenth; relating to the 14th day or the leap year context in old usage
examples: In bissext documents, the date is recorded as day fourteen.

senses.definition: leap year; relating to the extra day added every four years
examples: February has a bissextile day in a leap year.

senses.definition: plural of bissextile; leap years
examples: Most people forgot that some years are bissextiles.

senses.definition: the sixth day of February in ancient or liturgical contexts; related to leap year dating
examples: The term bissextus appears in old calendars.

senses.definition: a term used in some languages or contexts; may refer to a fibrous material or decorative tassel; usage is rare in English
examples: The bisso thread was used in embroidery.

senses.definition: a surname or place name; also a rare botanical term
examples: Dr. Bisson spoke at the conference.
Bisson Valley is a locality.

senses.definition: a plant or fossil genus name; used in scientific naming
examples: Bissonata is cited in the fossil record.

senses.definition: a brief stay or pause; in some contexts, a back-and-forth contrast in logic (less common today)
examples: He took a bist of time before replying.
